key_facts: Founded April 2013 by Huaxia Bank; solar leasing strategy launched 2015
By June 2023: CNY 32B+ (320亿) cumulative investment in residential PV leasing
By end-2023: CNY 39B+ (390亿) cumulative, 400K+ household stations
By end-2024: CNY 52B+ (520亿) invested, 14 GW installed, 500K+ stations, 250亿 kWh generated
Holds #1 market position among financial lessors in residential solar in China
Pioneer of 'zero-capex rooftop' financing model that unlocked rural solar adoption at scale

funding_history.event: Founded as subsidiary of Huaxia Bank
funding_history.notes: Bank-capitalized financial leasing company; capital raised via bond issuance and interbank funding, not VC

business_model_notes: Financial lessor model: Huaxia provides the capital for 100% upfront solar installation; household signs leasing contract (typically 20 years) paying monthly from electricity savings. Huaxia receives: (a) electricity revenue sharing or fixed lease payment, (b) REC/carbon credit revenues, (c) government FiT subsidies passed through. Key risk: rural household credit, grid connection delays, panel degradation. Scale advantage: financial institution's balance sheet can outgrow VC-backed solar startups.
competitive_position: #1 financial lessor for residential solar in China. Main competitors: Yuexiu New Energy (state-SASAC backed, CNY 16.1B in 2023 alone), other bank-affiliated leasing companies. Structurally different from independent solar companies — competes on cost of capital (Huaxia Bank's AAA rating = cheap funding).
research_notes: High confidence. CLBA (China Leasing Business Association) annual reports provide the primary data. Note: figures reported in 亿元 (100M CNY) — '320亿' = CNY 32B not 320B. This is important for cross-source comparison. End-2024 figures from solar.in-en.com report corroborated by company press releases.
